title: Restricted identifiers should not be used as identifiers
description: Identifiers in Java code should not use names that are reserved or restricted by the Java Language Specification, as this can lead to confusion, errors, or unexpected behavior.
recommendation: Avoid using Java reserved words or restricted identifiers as variable, method, class, or other symbol names.
impact: Using restricted identifiers can cause compilation errors or make code harder to read and maintain.

noncompliant: int enum = 5; // 'enum' is a restricted identifier in Java
class record { } // 'record' is a restricted identifier in Java

checks.message: Avoid using Java reserved words or restricted identifiers as variable, method, class, or other symbol names.
